habitat: Native pine woodland, plantation edges, hill slopes
lifecycle: Jan-Mar: Dormant. Apr-May: new needles form. May-Jun: male and female flowers. Jun-Oct: cones develop. Oct-Dec: cones ripen and release seeds.

habitat: Well-drained slopes, woodland edges, open ground
lifecycle: Jan-Mar: bare; catkins formed. Apr-May: leaves emerge, male catkins shed pollen. Jun-Aug: seeds develop. Sep-Oct: leaves yellow and fall. Nov-Dec: dormant.

habitat: Damp ground, heath edges, upland woodland
lifecycle: Similar to Silver Birch: spring leafing and catkins in Apr-May; seed ripening in late summer; leaf fall in autumn; winter dormant.

habitat: Woodland edges, rocky slopes, open hillside
lifecycle: Mar-Apr: buds burst. May-Jun: white flower clusters. Jul-Sep: red berries ripen. Oct: leaves turn red/orange and fall. Nov-Feb: berries often persist then are eaten by birds.
Feb-Mar: purple male catkins and small female cones. Apr-May: leaves emerge. Jun-Aug: seeds develop. Sep-Oct: seeds shed; leaves turn dull and fall. Winter: woody cones remain.
Jan-Mar: yellow catkins release pollen. Mar-Apr: red female flowers tiny on stems. Aug-Oct: nuts ripen. Oct-Nov: leaves yellow and fall. Winter: dormant.
Mar-Apr: white blossom before or with leaves. May-Jul: leaves fully out; fruits develop. Jul-Aug: cherries ripen. Sep-Oct: leaves turn red/orange and fall. Winter: dormant.
Apr-May: leaves and long drooping flower racemes appear. Jun-Jul: small black cherries form. Aug-Sep: fruits ripen; leaves yellow and fall. Winter dormant.
Feb-Mar: catkins produced. Apr-May: leaves open. Jun-Aug: seeds ripen and disperse. Sep-Oct: leaves turn golden and fall. Winter: dormant.
Evergreen. Apr-Jun: small white flowers. Aug-Dec: red berries develop and ripen; berries persist into winter, especially on female plants.
Apr-May: leaves and white blossom. Jun-Aug: haws (red fruits) develop. Sep-Oct: fruits ripen; leaves yellow and fall. Winter: bare.
May-Jun: flat clusters of creamy flowers. Jul-Sep: black berries ripen. Oct-Nov: leaves yellow and fall. Winter: bare.
Apr-May: leaves and catkins appear. Jun-Oct: acorns develop and ripen. Oct-Nov: leaf colour change and fall. Winter: dormant.
Evergreen; growth flush in spring; cones produced and ripen late summer to autumn.
Evergreen; cones develop over summer and ripen in autumn.
Evergreen; cones maturing late summer-autumn.
Mar-Apr: bright green needles appear. Apr-May: pinkish female flowers (larch roses). Sep-Oct: needles turn golden and fall; cones remain on branches over winter.
Jan-Mar: evergreen shoots. Apr-Jun: buds form. Jul-Sep: peak flowering. Sep-Nov: seed set. Nov-Dec: foliage persists.
Apr-May: shoots extend. Jun-Aug: bell-shaped flowers. Sep-Oct: seed capsules. Winter: evergreen.

habitat: Wet heath, boggy ground, peaty hollows
lifecycle: May-Jun: growth and bud formation. Jun-Aug: clusters of drooping pink bells. Sep-Oct: seed capsules. Winter: evergreen.

habitat: Pine and birch woodland, heath and moor edges
lifecycle: Mar-Apr: leaves emerge. Apr-Jun: small greenish-pink flowers. Jun-Aug: blue-black berries ripen. Sep-Oct: leaves redden and fall. Winter: stems bare.

habitat: Dry pine woodland, heaths, rocky slopes
lifecycle: Evergreen. Apr-Jun: white-pink bell flowers. Jul-Sep: bright red berries. Autumn-winter: berries may persist.
Evergreen. Apr-Jun: small purplish flowers. Jul-Sep: shiny black berries. Berries may persist into winter.
Evergreen. Apr-Jun: tiny flowers. Cones (‘berries’) take 2-3 years to ripen, usually blue-black by autumn of second/third year.
Evergreen. Feb-Jun: main flowering period with bright yellow flowers. Some flowers may appear almost year-round. Seed pods ripen and pop in summer.

habitat: Heathland margins, dry banks, open woodland edges
lifecycle: Apr-Jun: bright yellow flowers. Jun-Aug: seed pods developing and then bursting. Winter: bare green shoots.
Apr-Jun: leaves expand and flower buds form. Jun-Aug: white/pink flowers. Aug-Oct: blackberries ripen. Nov-Mar: canes persist, some leaves remain in mild winters.
Apr-May: leaves emerge. May-Jun: small white flowers. Jul-Aug: red raspberries ripen. Sep-Oct: canes die back after fruiting, new canes grow.

habitat: Wet heaths, bog margins, peaty hollows
lifecycle: Mar-Apr: catkins appear before leaves. May-Jun: leaves emerge. Summer: aromatic foliage. Autumn: leaves fall.

habitat: Shaded birch and mixed woodland floors
lifecycle: Jan-Mar: mostly dormant. Apr-Jun: leaves and white flowers appear. Jul-Aug: seed capsules form and eject seeds. Sep-Oct: leaves may persist. Nov-Dec: dies back.
Feb-Mar: leaves emerge. Mar-May: white star-like flowers open in sunshine. Jun-Jul: plants die back to underground rhizomes for rest of year.
Mar-Apr: leaves and early flowers. Apr-Jun: main flowering period. Summer: seed capsules. Autumn-winter: leaves may persist or die back.
Apr-May: leaves appear. May-Sep: yellow flowers. Sep-Oct: seeds ripen then plant dies back.

habitat: Damp meadows, wet heaths, grassy clearings
lifecycle: Apr-Jun: basal rosettes. Jul-Oct: lilac-blue button-like flower heads. Oct-Nov: seeds; foliage dies back over winter.

habitat: Grassland, path edges, open sunny slopes
lifecycle: Apr-May: new growth. May-Sep: yellow and orange 'eggs and bacon' flowers. Late summer: seed pods develop.
Apr-May: growth and bud formation. May-Aug: pale lilac-blue flowers. Autumn: dies back to creeping stems.
Apr-Jun: flowers begin. Jun-Sep: small pale blue or white flowers. Autumn-winter: creeping stems persist low to ground.
Apr-May: rosettes appear. May-Jul: pink tubular flowers. Aug-Sep: seed capsules. Winter: dies back.
Similar to Lousewort but flowering slightly later (Jun-Aug).
Apr-May: leaves appear. Jun-Oct: flat-topped clusters of white (sometimes pink) flowers. Autumn: seeds set; foliage may persist low into winter.
Apr-May: leaves and buds. May-Aug: purple flower clusters. Late summer: seed set; foliage persists low to ground.
Apr-May: shoots appear. May-Aug: deep blue or purple flowers. Late summer: fruits and seeds.
First year: rosette of leaves. Second year: May-Aug tall flower spike of purple bells. Autumn: seeds set; plant dies after seeding.
May-Jun: leaves and buds. Jun-Sep: nodding blue bell-flowers. Autumn: seed capsules; foliage dies back.

habitat: Deciduous woodland (more localised in NE Scotland)
lifecycle: Mar-Apr: leaves and buds. Apr-May: blue bell-shaped flowers on one-sided drooping spike. Jun: seeds; by July foliage dies back.

habitat: Damp meadows, stream edges, wet ditches
lifecycle: Apr-May: foliage appears. Jun-Aug: creamy flower plumes. Aug-Sep: seeds form. Winter: stems die back.
Mar-Apr: leaves and buds. Apr-May: bright yellow buttercup-like flowers. Jun-Jul: seed heads. Late summer-winter: dies back.

habitat: Acid bogs and very wet moorland
lifecycle: May-Jun: leaves and stems. Jul-Aug: spikes of golden-yellow starry flowers. Late summer: orange-brown seed heads.
Apr-May: sticky leaf rosette forms. May-Jul: violet flowers on stalks. Summer: leaves continue trapping insects. Autumn: forms winter bud and dies back above ground.
Apr-May: tightly coiled fiddleheads unfurl. Jun-Aug: fronds fully expanded. Sep-Oct: fronds turn brown and die back. Winter: underground rhizomes dormant.
Evergreen; new fronds appear in spring. May-Aug: upright fertile fronds produced; spores ripen and shed through summer and autumn.
Apr-Jun: new fronds unfurl. Summer: spores develop on underside. Late summer-autumn: spores shed; fronds die back.

habitat: Damp woodland, conifer plantations, shaded slopes
lifecycle: Similar to Male Fern: spring unfurling, summer spores, autumn die-back.

habitat: Moist woods, stream banks, shaded ravines
lifecycle: Apr-Jun: delicate fronds unfurl. Summer: spores develop and ripen. Autumn: fronds turn yellow/brown and die back.

habitat: Acidic upland woods, stream sides, damp slopes
lifecycle: Spring: new fronds appear. Summer: spores develop on undersides. Autumn: fronds die back.
Jul-Aug: fruiting bodies begin to appear. Aug-Nov: peak fruiting. Winter-spring: only underground mycelium persists.

habitat: Birch woodland and under solitary birches
lifecycle: Jul-Oct: fruiting bodies appear after rain; rest of year as mycelium linked to birch roots.
Jul-Oct: fruiting period; otherwise underground.
Jul-Oct: yellow fruiting bodies appear, often in patches; dormant as mycelium for rest of year.

habitat: On dead or dying birch trunks
lifecycle: Perennial bracket present year-round; new brackets appear mainly in late summer-autumn.

habitat: On dead hardwood logs and stumps
lifecycle: Fruiting brackets present most of year; growth strongest in warmer, wetter months.

habitat: On elder and other dead hardwood branches
lifecycle: Can fruit at almost any damp time, often autumn-spring.
Aug-Oct: puffballs appear and mature; spores released in puffs when squeezed or trodden on.

habitat: Pine and birch woodland floors, heaths
lifecycle: Evergreen; new 'steps' of growth added each year, most visible in spring and summer; spores in capsules when produced.
Evergreen carpets; sporophytes occasionally produced, mostly in growing season.

habitat: Wet heaths, bog edges, woodland rides
lifecycle: Evergreen; sporophyte stalks with capsules produced mainly spring-summer.
Active year-round in wet conditions; growth and peat formation continuous; sporophytes produced sporadically.
Present year-round; growth slow but continuous in suitable conditions.

habitat: On twigs and branches of trees, especially in clean air
lifecycle: Ever-present; grows slowly; may produce fruiting bodies in mature colonies.
Present year-round; slow-growing.

habitat: On tree branches, especially in humid woods
lifecycle: Evergreen; growth slow; fruiting bodies infrequent.
Evergreen mats; sporophytes occasional.
Evergreen; sporophytes occasional.
